sections.label: Lyrics
lines: The wind stands still, the sky leans low
A fire waits beneath the snow
We hold our breath in morning’s grip
Before the day begins to tip
I’ve seen the signs in distant fields
Where ashes speak, and silence yields
The earth remembers every name
But only love can clear the flame
The light is rising through the dark
It starts where you still bear the mark
A voice is calling from the edge
To turn, to mend, to make amends
Turn the hearts, turn the hearts
Of fathers to the children, let the broken play their part
Raise the voice, reclaim the spark
Before the fire meets the dark
Turn the hearts, turn the hearts
The prophet stands where time runs thin
A whisper louder than the wind
He speaks of grace, he warns with fire
But always leaves a thread of wire
To walk across from curse to light
To build a bridge from wrong to right
This is the moment, this is the cry
The echo that refuses to die
Turn the hearts, turn the hearts
Of fathers to the children, bring the scattered back to start
Let mercy fall, tear pride apart
Before the flames become too sharp
Turn the hearts, turn the hearts
Oh let the sons remember
Let the daughters rise
Let the elders teach the aching
What the fire purifies
Turn the hearts, turn the hearts
Of fathers to the children, and the children to the flame
Let healing be the legacy, not just the name
We were born to light the dark
So turn the hearts, turn the hearts
